title: Cost, efficiency, and outcomes of pulsed field ablation vs thermal ablation for atrial fibrillation
summary: Compares newer pulsed-field ablation with traditional heat-based techniques, showing how technology choice affects procedure time, cost, and patient outcomes.

title: Atrial fibrillation and stroke prevention: 25 years of research at EP Europace journal
summary: A quarter-century review of how stroke prevention in AF has evolved, from blood thinners to ablation and left atrial appendage closure.

title: Contemporary management of persistent atrial fibrillation
summary: A practical guide to treating persistent AF today: when to ablate, which energy to use, and how to reduce the chance of the rhythm returning.

title: Use of Ablation Index-Guided Ablation Results in High Rates of Durable Pulmonary Vein Isolation
summary: Shows that measuring lesion quality during ablation leads to more durable vein isolation and fewer repeat procedures for paroxysmal AF.

title: Predictors of Device-Related Thrombus Following Percutaneous Left Atrial Appendage Occlusion
journal: Journal of the American College of Cardiology
summary: Identifies which patients are at higher risk of blood clots forming on LAAO devices, helping doctors time anticoagulation more safely.

title: How to use digital devices to detect and manage arrhythmias: an EHRA practical guide
summary: European expert guidance on using smartwatches and phone apps to spot irregular heart rhythms and decide when further testing is needed.

title: Silent cerebral lesions following catheter ablation for atrial fibrillation: a state-of-the-art review
summary: Reviews the small brain changes sometimes seen on scans after AF ablation: what they mean, how common they are, and how to minimise risk.

title: Persistent and Recurrent Device-Related Thrombus After Left Atrial Appendage Closure
summary: Tracks how often clots form on or around LAAO devices over time, and which patients need longer blood-thinning after the procedure.

title: Outcomes of left atrial appendage occlusion vs. non-vitamin K antagonist oral anticoagulants
summary: Real-world comparison of closing the appendage versus taking newer blood thinners, helping patients who cannot tolerate long-term anticoagulation.

title: Clinical outcomes with very high power very short duration ablation for atrial fibrillation
summary: Tests a faster ablation technique that delivers high energy in brief bursts, with shorter procedure times and safety outcomes reported in detail.

title: Catheter ablation vs. thoracoscopic surgical ablation in long-standing persistent atrial fibrillation
summary: Head-to-head comparison of keyhole surgical ablation versus catheter ablation for the hardest-to-treat persistent AF patients.

title: Catheter ablation as first-line treatment for paroxysmal atrial fibrillation: a systematic review
summary: Synthesises the evidence for offering ablation early, before years of failed medications, in patients with intermittent AF.

title: Efficacy of Catheter Ablation for Persistent Atrial Fibrillation
summary: Landmark single-centre series showing that even persistent AF can be treated successfully with ablation when done by an experienced operator.

title: Ablation index, a novel marker of ablation lesion quality: prediction of pulmonary vein reconnection
summary: Introduces the Ablation Index, a measure of lesion quality that predicts whether veins stay isolated, reducing the need for repeat procedures.

title: Improvement in hard outcomes following catheter ablation for atrial fibrillation: the debate is far from over
summary: Examines whether ablation genuinely reduces stroke and heart failure, not just symptom relief, and where the evidence still has gaps.

title: Impact of early ablation of atrial fibrillation on long-term outcomes
summary: Follows patients over years to show that treating AF sooner, rather than waiting, is linked to better long-term heart health.

title: Quality of life and healthcare utilisation improvements after atrial fibrillation ablation
summary: Documents how successful ablation improves day-to-day wellbeing and reduces hospital visits, outcomes patients care about most.

title: Left Atrial Function Predicts Atrial Arrhythmia Recurrence Following Ablation of Long-Standing Persistent AF
summary: Uses heart imaging to predict which persistent-AF patients are most likely to stay in normal rhythm after ablation.

title: Incidence and predictors of stroke and silent cerebral embolism following very high-power short-duration ablation
summary: Reports stroke and silent brain-embolism rates after fast high-power ablation, identifying patient factors that raise or lower risk.

title: Cryoballoon Pulmonary Vein Isolation as First-Line Treatment for Typical Atrial Flutter
summary: Shows that freezing the pulmonary veins can treat atrial flutter as a first option, a less invasive alternative to conventional ablation.
